title: China-to-Argentina Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline Versus Argentina Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Argentina acceptance is built around the applicable legal route, IRAM / AEA or project standards, distributor or ENRE / CAMMESA grid review, local documentation, and site commissioning. China GB/T reports can be useful as technical capability evidence but are not Argentina legal approvals.
target_standards: Distributor, ENRE, or CAMMESA approval route
rows.china_equivalent: China baseline files commonly reference GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station grid connection, GB/T 37408-2019 for grid-connected PV inverter technical requirements, and NB/T 32004-2018 for grid-connected PV inverter technical specification. These should be translated into an Argentina traceability matrix.
rows.gap: Build a clause-by-clause bridge from GB/T / NB/T / IEC evidence to Argentina safety, installation, grid, labeling, language, and commissioning requirements. Mark any uncovered items before submitting to a distributor, ENRE / CAMMESA route, owner, or insurer.

mandatory_status.target: Argentina legal, distributor, ENRE / CAMMESA, project, and installation requirements are controlling for Argentina deployment
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence may be mandatory or project-specific in China, but is only supporting evidence for Argentina unless accepted by the Argentina approval route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T evidence is a baseline, not an Argentina substitute. Use it as traceable support for the local approval and commissioning file.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation of China (SAMR)

rows.target_requirement: Argentina PV inverter projects should be checked against local electrical-installation practice, including AEA low-voltage rules, AEA PV installation documents where specified, IRAM standards where referenced by the project, and provincial or municipal installer sign-off. These installation documents are normally enforced through the project, distributor, insurer, authority, or professional-signature route rather than by automatic acceptance of a foreign inverter certificate.
target_standards: AEA Reglamentacion para la Ejecucion de Instalaciones Electricas
AEA photovoltaic installation documents where specified
IRAM standards referenced by the project or authority
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin files may include GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004, IEC 62109 CB reports, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, Chinese wiring diagrams, and Chinese manuals. These are useful technical inputs but do not replace Argentina installation design, local installer qualification, or project-owner acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare an Argentina installation dossier: one-line diagrams, protection coordination, earthing, DC isolators, AC disconnects, surge protection, Spanish manuals, installer credentials, and a clause bridge from China / IEC reports to the local project specification.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by provincial or municipal electrical rules, distributor interconnection, insurer, project owner, or professional-signature process; individual IRAM / AEA documents may be voluntary unless incorporated by law, contract, or approval route
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T / NB/T / IEC evidence is supporting evidence only unless separately required by the Argentina project

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IRAM / AEA review as an Argentina installation and acceptance task. China inverter certificates should be mapped into the local dossier, not presented as a complete substitute.

rows.requirement: Secretaria de Energia User-Generator Rules and Electrical Safety File
rows.target_requirement: For distributed renewable generation under Ley 27.424, Argentina's official guidance points users to the Secretaria de Energia legislation set, including user-generator resolutions and implementation rules. The installation remains the responsibility of the interested user and must be performed by an authorized qualified installer, so inverter evidence should be packaged with local safety, installer, distributor, and user-generator documentation.
rows.china_equivalent: China user manuals, IEC 62109 reports, GB/T grid-function reports, and factory commissioning forms can support the safety file, but they do not complete the Argentina user-generator process or replace distributor platform documentation.
rows.gap: The gap is procedural and documentary: identify whether the project is a Ley 27.424 user-generator installation, confirm the applicable distributor or provincial route, and package inverter certificates with Spanish documentation, installer evidence, diagrams, settings, and platform submissions.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for installations using the Ley 27.424 distributed-generation user-generator route; otherwise project, distributor, provincial, or municipal requirements may control
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese safety and grid reports are not an Argentina user-generator authorization

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ley 27.424 projects need Argentina user-generator and distributor documentation in addition to inverter safety evidence.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Argentina Grid Profile and Protection Settings
rows.target_requirement: Argentina projects should document the inverter's local grid profile, including nominal 50 Hz operation, voltage and frequency trip windows, reconnection delay, anti-islanding, power-quality limits, export-limiting behavior, and any distributor or project-specific lockout settings. Nominal 50 Hz compatibility is only a starting point; acceptance depends on the approved settings at the connection point.
target_standards: Distributor interconnection settings for the connection point
ENRE / CAMMESA or project-specific grid studies where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses nominal 50 Hz grids, and China inverter reports commonly include 50 Hz operating data under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, and NB/T 32004. The issue is not the headline frequency but the Argentina-approved protection windows, firmware profile, and commissioning record.
rows.gap: Keep the China 50 Hz test evidence, but add an Argentina settings table, firmware screenshots, password or remote-change control, commissioning tests, and distributor or engineer sign-off.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a connection and commissioning requirement where the distributor, ENRE / CAMMESA process, engineer, or project owner specifies protection settings
mandatory_status.cn: Nominal 50 Hz evidence is useful but not a substitute for Argentina setting approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Argentina and China both use 50 Hz nominally, but the accepted protection profile must be documented for the Argentina connection point.

rows.requirement: ENRE / CAMMESA Grid Access for Utility or Transport-Connected PV
rows.target_requirement: Argentina federal electricity regulation and ENRE practice control access and expansion issues for federal-jurisdiction transmission and distribution concessions. For large PV projects or projects affecting transport capacity, inverter evidence is only one part of a grid-access file that may require ENRE resolutions, CAMMESA studies or procedures, transport capacity review, protection studies, commissioning records, and project-specific operating settings.
target_standards: ENRE access and transport-capacity resolutions where applicable
CAMMESA procedures and studies where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China grid files often cite GB/T 19964 for PV power-station grid connection, GB/T 37408 for grid-connected PV inverter technical requirements, NB/T 32004, and State Grid or China Southern Power Grid acceptance records. Those records do not authorize access to the Argentina grid.
rows.gap: Separate product capability from grid approval. The Argentina project file should include the authority route, interconnection study assumptions, inverter model settings, reactive-power capability, anti-islanding functions, fault ride-through data where requested, and commissioning sign-off.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the project requires ENRE, CAMMESA, transporter, distributor, or project-owner grid approval
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid reports are supporting technical evidence only

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] ENRE / CAMMESA grid acceptance is project-specific. China GB/T grid reports should be translated into the Argentina study package and settings file.

source.publisher: Ente Nacional Regulador de la Electricidad (ENRE)

rows.requirement: Ley 27.424 Distributed Generation and Distributor Connection
rows.target_requirement: Ley 27.424 establishes Argentina's national framework for renewable distributed generation for self-consumption with possible surplus injection to the distribution grid, and official Argentina guidance describes the program as a regime for self-consumption and grid injection. In practice, distributor enrollment, qualified installer work, platform documentation, meter arrangements, and provincial implementation status must be checked.
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic distributed-PV acceptance, State Grid connection records, and GB/T inverter tests can support technical due diligence but do not prove enrollment as an Argentina user-generator or distributor acceptance.
rows.gap: Before shipping, confirm whether the installation uses the national distributed-generation platform, a provincial route, or a distributor-specific process, then align inverter model, firmware profile, export limits, anti-islanding, labels, and Spanish manuals with that route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for projects claiming or using the Ley 27.424 user-generator route; otherwise distributor and provincial requirements still need separate confirmation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese distributed-PV acceptance is not recognized as Argentina distributor enrollment

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ley 27.424 is a deployment route, not just a product standard. The inverter file should support the distributor and user-generator process.
